Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze a set of process cases an event log against a normative process description to identify deviations violations and explain them

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Normative Process Description I need to extract the rules and constraints from the text

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Start Receive order is always the first step Step 2 Check inventory must occur after Receive order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Branching Logic Inventory Check Scenario A Sufficient Inventory Sequence Receive Check Authorize Payment Package items Ship order Close order Constraint Authorize payment only after Check inventory Constraint Package items only after Authorize payment Constraint Ship order only after Package items Constraint Ship order never after a cancellation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Scenario B Insufficient Inventory Sequence Receive Check Cancel order Close order Constraint After Cancel order no fulfillment activities Package Ship should follow Constraint Close order must be the final activity in all scenarios

Name: Validation - PE
Text: General Constraints Close order is the final activity Payment cannot happen before Inventory Check Packaging cannot happen before Payment Authorization Shipping cannot happen before Packaging Shipping cannot happen after Cancellation

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Event Log Case by Case I will trace the sequence of activities for each Case ID against the rules derived above

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Package items Ship order Close order Analysis Matches Scenario A Sufficient Inventory perfectly Verdict Valid

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Package items Authorize payment Ship order Close order Analysis Starts with Receive Yes Check before Package Yes Violation Package items occurs before Authorize payment Normative text says Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Verdict Violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 3 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Cancel order Ship order Close order Analysis Starts with Receive Yes Check before Cancel Yes Violation Cancel order occurs but Ship order follows it Normative text says Ship order should only occur after packaging and never after a cancellation Also after cancellation it is illogical to continue with fulfillment activities like shipping Secondary Note Package items is missing entirely in this case While the text implies a cancellation path skips packaging shipping the explicit violation is shipping after cancellation Verdict Violation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 4 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Cancel order Close order Analysis Matches Scenario B Insufficient Inventory perfectly Verdict Valid

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 5 Sequence Receive order Check inventory Authorize payment Ship order Close order Analysis Starts with Receive Yes Check before Payment Yes Violation Ship order occurs directly after Authorize payment Package items is missing Normative text says Package items should only occur after authorization and before shipping Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Verdict Violation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize the Findings Valid Cases 1 4 Violating Cases 2 3 5 Specific Violations Case 2 Packaging before Payment Authorization Case 3 Shipping after Cancellation and missing Packaging Case 5 Shipping without Packaging

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction Refinement In Case 3 the text says Once an order is canceled the next reasonable step is to Close order Case 3 does Ship before Close So Shipping after Cancel is the primary violation In Case 5 the text explicitly states Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order Case 5 skips packaging In Case 2 the text states Package items should only occur after authorization Case 2 does Package before Authorization

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait check Case 3 again It has Authorize payment before Cancel order The text implies if inventory is insufficient Cancel If sufficient Authorize Case 3 authorizes then cancels This is a bit ambiguous in the text However the text explicitly says Ship order never after a cancellation That is the hard rule Also Package items is skipped I will focus on the Ship after Cancel and Missing Package as clear violations of the flow logic

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait check Case 5 again It authorizes payment then ships It skips package The text says Once the payment is authorized the items should be prepared for shipment Only after items have been properly packaged is it appropriate to proceed with Ship order So skipping package is a violation

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Wait check Case 2 again It packages before authorizing Text Package items should only occur after authorization Violation confirmed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Wait check Case 1 and 4 Case 1 Receive Check Auth Package Ship Close Perfect match for Scenario A Case 4 Receive Check Cancel Close Perfect match for Scenario B

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Conclusion Cases 2 3 and 5 are the violations
